original: When 'debate' key is absent from config JSON, loadConfig() returns debate.enabled = false with all per-stage defaults populated (plan: stateful/synthesis/3, review: one-shot/majority/2, acceptance: one-shot/majority/1 disabled, rectification: one-shot/synthesis/1 disabled, escalation: one-shot/majority/1 disabled)
refined: When 'debate' key is absent from config JSON, loadConfig() returns debate.enabled = false with all per-stage defaults populated (plan: stateful/synthesis/3, review: one-shot/majority/2, acceptance: one-shot/majority/1 disabled, rectification: one-shot/synthesis/1 disabled, escalation: one-shot/majority/1 disabled)

original: When no debaters or resolver.agent is specified, no agent/model fields are stored in debate config — runtime resolves from config.autoMode.defaultAgent and config.models.fast
refined: When no debaters or resolver.agent is specified, no agent/model fields are stored in debate config — runtime resolves from config.autoMode.defaultAgent and config.models.fast

original: When a stage config has debaters array with fewer than 2 entries, NaxConfigSchema.safeParse() returns validation error containing 'debaters must have at least 2 entries'
refined: When a stage config has debaters array with fewer than 2 entries, NaxConfigSchema.safeParse() returns validation error containing 'debaters must have at least 2 entries'

original: When debate.stages.plan.resolver.type is set to 'invalid', NaxConfigSchema.safeParse() returns a validation error
refined: When debate.stages.plan.resolver.type is set to 'invalid', NaxConfigSchema.safeParse() returns a validation error

original: When debate.stages.plan is partially specified (e.g., only resolver), remaining fields fall back to that stage's built-in defaults (not global defaults)
refined: When debate.stages.plan is partially specified (e.g., only resolver), remaining fields fall back to that stage's built-in defaults (not global defaults)

original: When a debaters entry has agent but no model, the debater is valid — model is resolved at runtime
refined: When a debaters entry has agent but no model, the debater is valid — model is resolved at runtime

original: When nax config show is called, the debate section appears with human-readable descriptions for all fields
refined: When nax config show is called, the debate section appears with human-readable descriptions for all fields

original: DebateSession.run() resolves each debater's AgentAdapter via getAgent(debater.agent) and calls adapter.complete() with the debater's model override
refined: DebateSession.run() resolves each debater's AgentAdapter via getAgent(debater.agent) and calls adapter.complete() with the debater's model override

original: DebateSession.run() calls all debaters in parallel for the proposal round via Promise.allSettled()
refined: DebateSession.run() calls all debaters in parallel for the proposal round via Promise.allSettled()

original: When a debater's agent is not installed (getAgent returns null), that debater is skipped and a warning is logged with stage 'debate'
refined: When a debater's agent is not installed (getAgent returns null), that debater is skipped and a warning is logged with stage 'debate'

original: When fewer than 2 debaters succeed in the proposal round, DebateSession.run() falls back to single-agent mode — returns the one successful proposal or calls complete() fresh if all failed
refined: When fewer than 2 debaters succeed in the proposal round, DebateSession.run() falls back to single-agent mode — returns the one successful proposal or calls complete() fresh if all failed

original: When rounds is 2, the critique round sends each debater all other debaters' proposals in its critique prompt
refined: When rounds is 2, the critique round sends each debater all other debaters' proposals in its critique prompt

original: When rounds is 1 (propose only), critique round is skipped and proposals go directly to the resolver
refined: When rounds is 1 (propose only), critique round is skipped and proposals go directly to the resolver

original: majorityResolver() returns 'passed' when 2 of 3 proposals contain '"passed": true' in their JSON output
refined: majorityResolver() returns 'passed' when 2 of 3 proposals contain '"passed": true' in their JSON output

original: majorityResolver() returns the fail-closed answer (more conservative) on a tie (1 pass, 1 fail, 1 unparseable)
refined: majorityResolver() returns the fail-closed answer (more conservative) on a tie (1 pass, 1 fail, 1 unparseable)

original: synthesisResolver() calls adapter.complete() once with a synthesis prompt containing all proposals and critiques, returns the output
refined: synthesisResolver() calls adapter.complete() once with a synthesis prompt containing all proposals and critiques, returns the output

original: judgeResolver() calls adapter.complete() once with a judge prompt using resolver.agent (or default agent at fast tier if unset)
refined: judgeResolver() calls adapter.complete() once with a judge prompt using resolver.agent (or default agent at fast tier if unset)

original: DebateResult.totalCostUsd equals the sum of all complete() call costs across all debaters and rounds including resolver
refined: DebateResult.totalCostUsd equals the sum of all complete() call costs across all debaters and rounds including resolver

original: DebateResult.proposals contains the debater identity (agent, model) alongside each proposal output
refined: DebateResult.proposals contains the debater identity (agent, model) alongside each proposal output

original: When sessionMode is 'stateful', DebateSession.run() creates a SpawnAcpClient per debater and calls client.createSession() with session name containing storyId and debater index
refined: When sessionMode is 'stateful', DebateSession.run() creates a SpawnAcpClient per debater and calls client.createSession() with session name containing storyId and debater index

original: When sessionMode is 'stateful', critique round does NOT paste all proposals into the prompt — sends only other debaters' proposals (session retains own history)
refined: When sessionMode is 'stateful', critique round does NOT paste all proposals into the prompt — sends only other debaters' proposals (session retains own history)

original: When sessionMode is 'stateful', all sessions are closed via session.close() in the finally block — even if debate fails mid-round
refined: When sessionMode is 'stateful', all sessions are closed via session.close() in the finally block — even if debate fails mid-round

original: When sessionMode is 'one-shot', DebateSession.run() uses adapter.complete() and does NOT create persistent sessions
refined: When sessionMode is 'one-shot', DebateSession.run() uses adapter.complete() and does NOT create persistent sessions

original: When a stateful session fails to create (acpx error), that debater is skipped and debate continues with remaining debaters (minimum 2 required)
refined: When a stateful session fails to create (acpx error), that debater is skipped and debate continues with remaining debaters (minimum 2 required)

original: When two stages in the same run have different sessionMode values (e.g., plan=stateful, review=one-shot), each uses its own mode independently
refined: When two stages in the same run have different sessionMode values (e.g., plan=stateful, review=one-shot), each uses its own mode independently

original: When debate.enabled is true and debate.stages.plan.enabled is true, nax plan --auto uses DebateSession.run() instead of single adapter.complete()
refined: When debate.enabled is true and debate.stages.plan.enabled is true, nax plan --auto uses DebateSession.run() instead of single adapter.complete()

original: When debate.enabled is false, nax plan --auto calls adapter.complete() exactly once (no behavior change)
refined: When debate.enabled is false, nax plan --auto calls adapter.complete() exactly once (no behavior change)

original: When debate.enabled is true and debate.stages.review.enabled is true, runSemanticReview() uses DebateSession.run() instead of single agent.complete()
refined: When debate.enabled is true and debate.stages.review.enabled is true, runSemanticReview() uses DebateSession.run() instead of single agent.complete()

original: When review debate uses majority resolver, final ReviewCheckResult.success reflects the majority vote of debater responses
refined: When review debate uses majority resolver, final ReviewCheckResult.success reflects the majority vote of debater responses

original: When review debate uses majority resolver, ReviewCheckResult.findings contains merged findings deduplicated by AC id from all debaters
refined: When review debate uses majority resolver, ReviewCheckResult.findings contains merged findings deduplicated by AC id from all debaters

original: When all debaters fail during plan, system falls back to single adapter.complete() and logs warning with stage 'debate' and event 'fallback'
refined: When all debaters fail during plan, system falls back to single adapter.complete() and logs warning with stage 'debate' and event 'fallback'

original: When debate.stages.rectification.enabled is true, rectification loop runs debate session to diagnose root cause BEFORE building the agent's rectification prompt
refined: When debate.stages.rectification.enabled is true, rectification loop runs debate session to diagnose root cause BEFORE building the agent's rectification prompt

original: Diagnosis output is prepended to the rectification prompt as a '## Root Cause Analysis' section
refined: Diagnosis output is prepended to the rectification prompt as a '## Root Cause Analysis' section

original: When debate.stages.rectification.enabled is false (default), rectification loop is unchanged from today
refined: When debate.stages.rectification.enabled is false (default), rectification loop is unchanged from today

original: When diagnosis debate fails (all debaters error), rectification prompt proceeds without diagnosis section and logs event 'fallback'
refined: When diagnosis debate fails (all debaters error), rectification prompt proceeds without diagnosis section and logs event 'fallback'

original: Debate cost is included in the story's total cost tracking
refined: Debate cost is included in the story's total cost tracking
